text:
## Team Context

**Your assigned name**: spec-analyst-opensource
**Team Lead**: team-lead (use SendMessage to reach me)

**Communication Protocol** (you have SendMessage in your tools):
- `QUESTION: ...` -- before starting, if requirements have genuine ambiguity
- `BLOCKER: ...` -- immediately if you cannot proceed
- `DONE: {summary}` -- when your deliverables are complete
- `SUGGESTION: ...` -- proactively flag tech issues, arch concerns, refactoring ideas

**Context Strategy**: repomix
All relevant context is pre-loaded below. Use Read/Glob/Grep for additional files.

**Escalation rule**: Do NOT work silently on ambiguity. Ask first.

---

## Task

You are the OPEN-SOURCE ANALYST for Notesaner. Your job is to identify EVERY open-source library, package, tool, and code pattern that can be borrowed or adapted to accelerate development. The goal is to avoid reinventing the wheel wherever possible.

### Read These Files First
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/requirements.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/proposed-features.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/tech-stack.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/architecture/overview.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/domains/` -- all files
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/research/note-app-pain-points-report.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/research/obsidian-deep-dive.md`

### Deliverables

Write a COMPREHENSIVE open-source audit document to:
`/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/artifacts/wave-01-specs/04-opensource-audit.md`

Cover EVERY domain and identify what can be reused:

#### 1. Editor (TipTap/ProseMirror Ecosystem)
- List EVERY relevant TipTap extension (official and community)
- Identify which of our R1 requirements already have TipTap extensions
- Note which extensions need customization vs direct use
- Find TipTap starter kits or editor templates to base on
- Identify ProseMirror plugins that can be wrapped as TipTap extensions
- Specific packages:
  - @tiptap/extension-* (list all relevant ones)
  - tiptap-markdown
  - @tiptap-pro/* (evaluate if needed, they have a free tier for OSS)
  - prosemirror-math
  - Any wiki-link extensions
  - Any callout/admonition extensions

#### 2. Yjs/CRDT Ecosystem
- y-websocket (server + client)
- y-indexeddb
- y-protocols (awareness, auth)
- y-prosemirror
- Hocuspocus (alternative Yjs server -- evaluate)
- @tiptap/extension-collaboration
- @tiptap/extension-collaboration-cursor
- Any Yjs server reference implementations

#### 3. Auth Libraries
- passport-saml configurations and examples
- openid-client setup patterns
- @nestjs/jwt patterns
- bcrypt vs argon2 comparison
- otplib (TOTP implementation)
- next-auth vs custom auth patterns
- CASL or nest-casl for RBAC

#### 4. NestJS Ecosystem
- @nestjs/cache-manager
- @nestjs/bullmq
- @nestjs/throttler
- @nestjs/websockets
- @nestjs/config
- @nestjs/swagger (API docs)
- nestjs-pino
- nestjs-prisma
- Any NestJS boilerplate projects to reference

#### 5. UI Components
- shadcn/ui -- complete component list and which we need
- cmdk (command palette -- used by shadcn/ui)
- react-resizable-panels (for split panes)
- @dnd-kit (drag and drop patterns)
- Virtualized lists (react-window, @tanstack/react-virtual)
- File tree components (existing React file explorer components)
- react-hotkeys-hook (keyboard shortcuts)
- Lucide icons list needed

#### 6. Graph Visualization
- d3-force options and examples
- @react-three/fiber for WebGL
- force-graph (a wrapper library)
- react-force-graph
- sigma.js (alternative)
- Any knowledge graph visualization examples

#### 7. Markdown Processing
- unified/remark/rehype ecosystem
- remark plugins for wiki links
- remark-gfm, remark-math, remark-frontmatter
- rehype-highlight, rehype-katex
- mdast-util-wiki-link (or similar)
- Any Obsidian-compatible markdown parsers

#### 8. Built-in Plugin Dependencies
For EACH built-in plugin, identify the core library:
- Excalidraw: @excalidraw/excalidraw
- Kanban: any React kanban libraries
- Calendar: FullCalendar or alternatives
- Database: TanStack Table
- Slides: reveal.js or alternatives
- AI: vercel/ai SDK or LangChain
- PDF Export: jspdf, html2pdf, or Puppeteer
- Mermaid: mermaid.js
- Spaced Repetition: SM-2 algorithm implementations

#### 9. Open-Source Note Apps to Study
Identify open-source apps similar to Notesaner that can be studied for patterns:
- AFFiNE (Block editor + whiteboard)
- SilverBullet (self-hosted note app)
- Outline (team wiki)
- BookStack (documentation platform)
- Hedgedoc (collaborative markdown)
- Trilium Notes (knowledge base)
- Docmost (wiki)
- Any others relevant

For each, list:
- GitHub URL
- License compatibility with AGPL-3.0
- Specific code/patterns that could be adapted
- What we should NOT copy (architectural mistakes)

#### 10. DevOps Tools
- Docker multi-stage build patterns
- GitHub Actions reusable workflows
- NX cloud alternatives
- Monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana)
- Error tracking (Sentry)

#### 11. Testing Tools
- Vitest patterns and configuration
- Playwright best practices
- MSW (Mock Service Worker) for API mocking
- Testing Library patterns
- Faker.js for test data
- Prisma test database patterns

#### 12. Acceleration Recommendations
For each domain, estimate:
- % of work saved by using open-source
- Effort to integrate vs build from scratch
- Maintenance burden of dependencies
- License compatibility assessment

Provide a summary table:
| Feature | Build from Scratch (days) | With Open-Source (days) | Key Package | License |

Send me DONE when complete with: document path and estimated development time savings.
summary: Spawn open-source analyst for reuse audit
